channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: China’s Big Bet on Wind Power Is Paying Off
item.description: An industrial policy of subsidies and import restrictions laid the foundations for China to become almost as dominant in wind turbines as in solar panels.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher and Qilai Shen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: What We Know About ‘Project Freedom’ in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Violence flared in the waterway on Monday after President Trump said the United States would help guide stranded ships. It’s not clear exactly how the plan is working.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz near Musandam, Oman, last week. Some 1,600 vessels are believed to be stranded.

item.title: The Growing Rift Between the UAE and Saudi Arabia, Explained
item.description: Officials say competition between the countries is healthy. But tensions are mounting over energy quotas, regional conflicts and their different visions for the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.media:credit: Bandar Algaloud/Saudi Royal Court
item.media:description: A photograph released by Saudi Royal Court showing Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, right, and the Emirati leader, Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed, in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, in 2023.

item.title: Kennedy Starts a Push to Help Americans Quit Antidepressants
item.description: The health secretary has long complained that Americans overuse psychiatric medications. New policies he is introducing aim to change that.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Barry
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Monday’s announcements were Health Secretary Kennedy’s first significant step to reduce the use of antidepressants.

item.title: U.S. Trade Deficit Grew in March
item.description: Both exports and imports rose after the Supreme Court struck down many of the president’s highest levies in February.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Ruth Fremson/The New York Times
item.media:description: The data provided the first snapshot of trade since a ruling by the Supreme Court forced major changes to the Trump administration’s tariffs.

item.title: These 7 Primary Elections Will Test President Trump’s Power in Indiana
item.description: The president is seeking political payback for Republican state legislators in Indiana who rejected his push to redraw the state’s political map last year.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Jon Cherry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Indiana state legislators met in Indianapolis last year before a vote on redrawing the state’s congressional map.

item.title: President Trump Seeks Retribution in Republican Primaries
item.description: President Trump is endorsing candidates in a series of primary elections this month, trying to defeat incumbents in his own party who have crossed him. Our national political correspondent Shane Goldmacher explains.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher, Jon Miller, Paul Abowd and Luke Piotrowski

item.title: Paxton’s Fund-Raising Struggles in Texas Underscore Deep Rift in G.O.P.
item.description: The Texas attorney general is heading into the runoff against Senator John Cornyn without the support of some of his longtime backers.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: On the campaign trail for U.S. Senate, the Texas attorney general, Ken Paxton, has presented his fund-raising gap as a strength.

item.title: A Top 2026 Senate Race Kicks Off With Attacks About Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Ohio’s unusual contest — with a little-known incumbent and a well-known challenger — shows how Democrats are hoping to capitalize on G.O.P. voters’ anger at the Jeffrey Epstein scandal.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times; Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Former Senator Sherrod Brown of Ohio, left, is running again for the chamber against Senator Jon Husted, a Republican who was appointed to his seat by the governor to replace Vice President JD Vance.

item.title: Our 15 Favorite Looks at the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: Regardless of the theme, these people just looked great.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk
item.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times

item.title: Mingling With Jeff Bezos and Stevie Nicks at the 2026 Met Gala Cocktail Party
item.description: An exclusive glimpse inside the party, where guests had many interpretations for the “Fashion Is Art” dress code.
item.dc:creator: Sandra E. Garcia and Landon Nordeman
item.media:description: Blue Ivy Carter and Beyoncé making their entrance.

item.title: Secret Recordings, Hidden Shares and a Family Rift at the South Korean Giant LG
item.description: An inheritance dispute over the former chairman’s estate has prompted a criminal complaint over how the dynastic chaebol family behind LG divides its assets.
item.dc:creator: Daisuke Wakabayashi and Grace Moon
item.media:credit: Illustration by Max-o-matic. Photographs by Getty Images, Associated Press, Chang W. Lee/The New York Times, Shutterstock.

item.title: The Federal Safety Net Isn’t Ready for Artificial Intelligence
item.description: As fears of A.I.-driven job losses mount, economists warn that unemployment benefits and other programs to help displaced workers aren’t sufficient.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman and Tony Romm
item.media:credit: Felix Decombat

item.title: Five Publishers and Scott Turow Sue Meta and Mark Zuckerberg
item.description: The class-action lawsuit accuses the tech giant and its founder and chief executive of infringing on authors’ copyrights.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Alter
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Mark Zuckerberg in March 2026.

item.title: U.S.C. Will Infuse A.I. Across University with $200 Million Donation
item.description: The University of Southern California wants to integrate artificial intelligence into fields like health care and the arts. Other universities are also using donations to focus more on A.I.
item.dc:creator: Alan Blinder
item.media:credit: Kendrick Brinson for The New York Times
item.media:description: The University of Southern California campus in Los Angeles.

item.title: When ‘The Late Show With Stephen Colbert’ Goes Away, What Do We Lose?
item.description: Our attachment to an institution may seem counterintuitive, especially with comedy, a rebellious art form. But with Stephen Colbert’s program, there was a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Celina Pereira; Photographs, via Getty Images

item.title: What a Bike Ride in Cape Town Taught Me About Apartheid
item.description: A New York Times reporter joined a group of cyclists on a route meant to break down Cape Town’s lingering racial and economic barriers.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Tommy Trenchard
item.media:credit: Tommy Trenchard for The New York Times

item.title: Can Javier Milei’s Deregulatory Frenzy Buoy the Global Right?
item.description: With his signature chain saw, Javier Milei became a MAGA superstar. At home, his state-slashing experiment is on the line.
item.dc:creator: Roger Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: New Musicals ‘Schmigadoon!’ and ‘Lost Boys’ Lead List of Tony Nominees
item.description: The nominated performers include the film stars Daniel Radcliffe, Rose Byrne, John Lithgow and Lesley Manville.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
item.media:description: “Schmigadoon!” is adapted from a comedic Apple TV series and received 12 Tony nominations, tied with “The Lost Boys” for the most.

item.title: Tony Awards Nominations 2026: The Complete List
item.description: Nominations for the 79th Tony Awards were announced on Tuesday. Here’s who made the list.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Sherman
item.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
item.media:description: A scene from “The Lost Boys,” which earned 12 Tony nominations including best new musical.

item.title: Tony Nominations Snubs and Surprises: Lea Michele Left Out, ‘Titaníque’ Shines
item.description: Lea Michele, Adrien Brody and other boldface names were left out, while June Squibb, André De Shields and Layton Williams as an iceberg were among the surprises.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw, Jesse Green, Alexis Soloski, Scott Heller, Elisabeth Vincentelli and Laura Collins-Hughes
item.media:credit: Photographs by Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
item.media:description: Lea Michele and Nicholas Christopher in “Chess,” left, and André De Shields in “Cats: The Jellicle Ball.”

item.title: Cruise Ship Struck by Hantavirus Is to Head to Canary Islands, W.H.O. Says
item.description: Spain said it would decide on a port of call only after disease experts inspected the vessel. The World Health Organization said human-to-human transmission may have played a role in the outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim, Lynsey Chutel and Claire Moses
item.media:credit: Arilson Almeida/Associated Press
item.media:description: The MV Hondius moored off the coast of Cape Verde, an archipelago in the Atlantic Ocean, on Monday.

item.title: How a Hantavirus Infection Feels: ‘You Just Can’t Get the Air In’
item.description: Doctors say that the virus, which is rare, can quickly progress from flulike symptoms to something far more serious.
item.dc:creator: Simar Bajaj and Nina Agrawal

item.title: What to Know About the Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
item.description: Health authorities said three passengers from the MV Hondius died after showing symptoms of the rare disease, with another critically ill and three more showing mild symptoms.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young and Claire Moses
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The cruise ship MV Hondius stationed off the port of Praia, Cape Verde, on Sunday.

item.title: A Portfolio of Work From The Times’s Pulitzer Prize-Winning Photographer
item.description: The photographer Saher Alghorra reported on the Gaza conflict while enduring the dire conditions his images portrayed.
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Displaced Palestinians began returning to the north of Gaza after the Israeli military announced that a cease-fire was in effect.

item.title: New York Times Wins 3 Pulitzer Prizes
item.description: The Washington Post won the award for public service, considered the most prestigious of the Pulitzers, for its coverage of the Trump administration’s overhaul of federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Katie Robertson
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: A photo that was part of the Pulitzer Prize-winning entry by Saher Alghorra, a contributor to The New York Times, in the breaking news photo category. It showed a child wounded in Gaza City being transferred to a hospital in April last year.

item.title: Pulitzer Prizes: 2026 Winners List
item.description: Here is the full list of winners and finalists.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: This image, showing displaced Palestinians walking to Gaza City in October 2025, was part of the entry by Saher Alghorra, a contributor to The New York Times who won for breaking news photography.

item.title: This Trump Proposal is Good for Private Equity — and Not Good for Our Retirement Accounts
item.description: A win for private equity. A loss for our retirement assets.
item.dc:creator: Steven Rattner

item.title: The Book That Changed How I Think About Liberalism
item.description: The historian Helena Rosenblatt traces the roots of liberalism and the values that many have forgotten.
item.dc:creator: Ezra Klein and Jack McCordick
item.category: The Lost History of Liberalism: From Ancient Rome to the Twenty-First Century (Book)
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: The Best Looks From the Met Gala
item.description: We’re covering the cobblestone carpet.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: The New York Times
item.media:description: Beyoncé and Blue Ivy, her daughter.

item.title: Fireworks Factory Explosion in China Kills at Least 26
item.description: Dozens more were injured in the explosion, which appeared to be one of the country’s deadliest in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.media:credit: Cnsphoto, via Reuters
item.media:description: The site of an explosion at a fireworks factory in Liuyang, China, on Monday. China has improved its record on industrial accidents in recent years, but fireworks factories remain a vulnerability.

item.title: Secret Service Agent John Spillman Charged With Indecent Exposure at Miami Hotel
item.description: The officer, who was in Miami for an event President Trump was attending over the weekend, was seen masturbating in the hallway, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra and Sonia A. Rao

item.title: The Uncertain Fate of France’s Last Two Captive Orcas
item.description: France must decide what to do with two orcas marooned at a deserted marine park: move them to another park, or release them into an open-water sanctuary.
item.dc:creator: Ana Castelain
item.media:credit: Valery Hache/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Keijo, an orca, in November at the Marineland park in France. Because he and his mother were born in captivity, experts say they would struggle in the open sea.

item.title: Rare Comet Appears Over New Zealand, Australia and South Africa
item.description: Known as C/2025 R3 PANSTARRS, the comet will be visible to stargazers using a telescope in New Zealand, Australia and South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung
